howItWorks.intro: This solar still output calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Output = Area (sqft) x Sun Hours x Moisture Factor x 8 oz
A 3-foot still produces roughly 8-32 oz/day depending on conditions
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much water does a solar still produce?
faq.answer: A well-built 3-foot diameter solar still produces 8-32 oz per day depending on soil moisture, sun, and temperature. In ideal conditions with added vegetation or contaminated water, output improves. You typically need 3-5 stills to supplement one person's needs.

faq.question: How do I build a solar still?
faq.answer: Dig a 3-foot wide, 2-foot deep hole. Place a container in the center. Cover with clear plastic sheeting, seal edges with soil. Place a small rock in the center of the plastic above the container. Moisture evaporates, condenses on plastic, and drips into the container.

faq.question: Is the Solar Still Output Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What still diameter should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate still diameter value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Solar Still Output Calculator?
sections.body: The Solar Still Output Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your still diameter, peak sun hours, soil moisture, you get instant results including collection area, output per still/day, total daily output. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting collection area right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your still diameter and need to find the right collection area. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Still Diameter (inches)

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your collection area, output per still/day, total daily output. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your still diameter before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
